name: CARDIAC SARCOMA
definition: OSTEOSARCOMA OF HEART = CARDIAC OSTEOSARCOMA PLEOMORPHIC SARCOMA = ROUND CELL SARCOMA = SPINDLE CELL SARCOMA

demographics.Mean age: 41 years; extremely rare in infants + children
demographics.Frequency: most common cardiac malignancy of adulthood
demographics.Path: frequently hemorrhagic + necrotic mass, often adherent to pericardium → pericardial
demographics.Histo: mesenchymal cells characterized by ill-defined vascular spaces lined by atypical
demographics.Location: right atrial free wall + involvement of pericardium (80%)

dominant_finding: central areas of low SI ← tumor necrosis
distribution: right atrial free wall + involvement of pericardium (80%)
discriminating_features: contrast enhancement, areas of hemorrhage, central necrosis
central areas of low SI ← tumor necrosis

general: contrast enhancement, areas of hemorrhage, central necrosis
PET: difficult identification of sarcoma if myocardial FDG activity is high
usually originating from RA with sparing of atrial septum
areas of central necrosis communicating with cardiac chamber
disruption of fat planes, pericardial thickening / nodularity
"cauliflower appearance" = local nodular predominantly hyperintense areas
obliterated pericardial space (hemorrhage + necrotic tumor debris)
"sunray appearance" = linear contrast enhancement along vascular lakes on MR
iso- to hypointense on T1WI + hyperintense on T2WI
broad base of attachment with invasive behavior
MR: central areas of low SI ← tumor necrosis
thickening / irregularity of myocardium ← tumor infiltration
hemorrhagic mass replacing the pericardium (similar to angiosarcoma)

clinical.Prognosis: mean survival of 3 month to 1 year
